title: Fire Attack on a Media Office in London
summary: Three people have been charged for trying to start a fire at a Persian language media office in London. They threw a burning container at the building, but it landed in a car park and the fire went out quickly. No one was hurt, and no damage was done. The police chased a car that crashed, and the three people are now going to court. This happened after other similar attacks in the area, including at a synagogue and on ambulances. The police say there is no bigger danger to the public.

background_read: In London, some people have been trying to start fires at buildings like media offices and synagogues. The police are working to stop these attacks and keep everyone safe. Arson is a serious crime because it can hurt people and damage property. It's important to report any suspicious activities to the police.
Article_Structure: WHO: Three people, Oisin McGuinness, Nathan Dunn, and a 16-year-old boy, who are British nationals.
WHAT: They were charged with arson for throwing a burning container at a Persian media office, but no one was hurt.
WHERE: At the Volant Media building in Park Royal, north-west London.
WHY: This is part of a series of attacks in the area, including on a synagogue and ambulances, and the police are investigating to prevent more crimes.

perspectives.description: The police are working to protect people from dangerous attacks in the community.
